task_completion.description: Navigate to the Perito Moreno Glacier terminus location
task_completion.visual_evidence: Map view showing the Perito Moreno Glacier terminus (where the ice front meets the water). Coordinates near -50.497, -73.137 should be visible in the status bar or search.

task_completion.description: Trace the ice/water boundary with a path tool
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible vector line or path drawn along the front edge of the glacier where it meets the lake.
The 'Places' sidebar or path properties dialog showing the path item named exactly 'Perito_Moreno_Terminus'.

task_completion.description: Verify the measured length is between 4 km and 6 km
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Measurements' tab or properties window for the path displaying a length/distance value between 4.0 km and 6.0 km (or 4000-6000 meters).

task_completion.description: Create a placemark named 'Terminus_Center' at the midpoint
task_completion.visual_evidence: A pushpin or placemark icon visible on the map near the center of the drawn path, labeled 'Terminus_Center'.

task_completion.description: Record the measured width in the placemark description
task_completion.visual_evidence: The properties dialog for the 'Terminus_Center' placemark showing the measured width value (e.g., '5.1 km') in the 'Description' field.
A 'Save As' dialog or file manager view showing the file 'glacier_terminus.kml' being saved to or existing in the 'Documents' folder.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used a GIS/Map application interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the UI of a mapping application (e.g., Google Earth Pro, Marble) rather than a script or text editor generating the KML programmatically.

integrity.description: Ensure the recorded width matches the tool's measurement
integrity.visual_evidence: The value typed into the placemark description matches the length value shown in the measurement tool or path properties in the screenshots.

integrity.description: Ensure the path follows the irregular coastline
integrity.visual_evidence: The path line consists of multiple points following the curve of the glacier front, rather than a single straight line segment.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Perito Moreno Glacier terminus is located at approximately -50.497, -73.137. The scientific width of the terminus is approximately 5.0 km. Any measurement between 4.0 km and 6.0 km is considered acceptable.
